data.title: DeepSeek to use Huawei chips for new V4 model: report
data.description:
China's DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) will use chips designed by Huawei Technologies to run its new model V4, The Information reported, citing five people with direct ​knowledge of ​the ⁠purchase.

Ahead of V4’s launch, Chinese tech giants including Alibaba Group, ByteDance, and Tencent Holdings have reportedly placed bulk orders for Huawei’s upcoming chips, totaling hundreds of thousands of units.

The next-generation model is likely to be launched in the coming weeks. DeepSeek is also developing two additional V4 variants, each tailored for different capabilities and designed to run on Chinese-made chips, the report said.

The Hangzhou-based AI startup has spent the past few months working directly with Huawei and another Chinese chip designer, Cambricon Technologies, to rewrite and test the model’s underlying code, as per the report that cited two people close to the company.

data.title: Meta Platforms and Google face jury setbacks; Section 230 under pressure
data.description:
A major legal shift may be underway for Big Tech companies, as protections under Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act come under pressure.

Last week, juries in Los Angeles and New Mexico reportedly delivered key verdicts against Meta Platforms (META) and Google (GOOGL).

In Los Angeles, jurors reportedly found META and YouTube negligent in a personal injury case, awarding ~$6M to a young woman who began using the apps as a child.

Meanwhile, in New Mexico, the jury ordered Meta Platforms (META) to pay $375M for failing to protect minors from child predators. Attorney General Raúl Torrez said he will seek further penalties and push for app changes to improve safety, CNBC reported.

Plaintiffs argued that design features like autoplay, recommendation algorithms, and notifications in Instagram and YouTube acted like “digital casinos,” driving addictive use and mental harm of minors.

Alongside this, new lawsuits are also emerging around AI tools, including claims that Google's (GOOGL) systems exposed sensitive personal data. Lawmakers are also raising pressures, as Senator Brian Schatz said tech firms have long relied on Section 230 as a shield to avoid action because fixing harms could hurt profits.

Both the companies said they plan to appeal. Legal expert David Greene called the verdicts “very preliminary decisions,” adding that simply labeling something as a design feature does not remove potential legal protections.

data.title: Top 10 small-cap energy stocks ranked by dividend yield
data.description:
As the market grapples with volatility, below is a list of the top 10 small-cap energy stocks based in the United States, ranked by their forward dividend yield.

The list is topped by CrossAmerica Partners LP (CAPL), with a dividend yield of 10.08%. Kimbell Royalty Partners, LP (KRP) and Vitesse Energy, Inc. (VTS) are next, with TXO Partners, L.P. (TXO) and Granite Ridge Resources, Inc. (GRNT) rounding out the rest of the top five. Diversified Energy Company (DEC), Global Partners LP (GLP) and Riley Exploration Permian, Inc. (REPX) are among the other high-yielding stocks on the list.

The list concludes with VAALCO Energy, Inc. (EGY) and World Kinect Corporation (WKC), which offer dividend yields of 3.99% and 3.41%, respectively.

Here is the list:

  1. CrossAmerica Partners LP Common Units (CAPL), Dividend yield: 10.08%

  2. Kimbell Royalty Partners, LP Common Units (KRP), Dividend yield: 10.03%

  3. Vitesse Energy, Inc. (VTS), Dividend yield: 9.67%

  4. TXO Partners, L.P. Common Units (TXO), Dividend yield: 9.58%

  5. Granite Ridge Resources, Inc. (GRNT), Dividend yield: 7.68%

  6. Diversified Energy Company (DEC), Dividend yield: 6.79%

  7. Global Partners LP Common Units (GLP), Dividend yield: 6.76%

  8. Riley Exploration Permian, Inc. (REPX), Dividend yield: 4.37%

  9. VAALCO Energy, Inc. (EGY), Dividend yield: 3.99%

  10. World Kinect Corporation (WKC), Dividend yield: 3.41%

data.title: Catalyst Watch: OPEC meeting, FedEx talks freight, inflation reads, and SpaceX IPO buzz
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

Sunday - April 5

  • Oil watch - OPEC+ will hold a meeting. The key issue is whether the group sticks with a modest output increase or surprises traders with a larger move. The decision matters because it could swing crude prices, shift inflation expectations, and ripple into energy stocks and the broader market.

Monday - April 6

  • Volatility watch - CoreWeave (CRWV) and Cytokinetics (CYTK) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Kore Group (KORE), Apellis Pharma (APLS), and Tilly’s (TLYS). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Service Properties (SVC), Sanuwave Health (SNWV), and MDxHealth SA (MDXH). Short interest is elevated on Sphere Entertainment (SPHR) and Serve Robotics (SERV) again.
  • IPO watch - No IPOs are expected to price next week and begin to trade. The IPO lockup periods expire on blocks of shares of Alliance Laundry (ALH), Phoenix Education Partners (PXED), and Leifras (LFS). The quiet period ends on PayPay (PAYP) to free up analysts to post ratings. The countdown for investors to see SpaceX's (SPACE) financials ahead of a planned June IPO will continue.
  • All day - Powell Industries (POWL) will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis following the three-for-one stock split.
  • All day - The HumanX AI Conference in San Francisco will include participation from Amazon Web Services (AMZN), Anthropic, Canva, Conviction, CoreWeave (CRWV), Databricks, DeepLearning.AI, Google (GOOGL), Khosla Ventures, Mercedes-Benz (MBGAF), Microsoft (MSFT), NVIDIA (NVDA), OpenAI (OPENAI), and Perplexity.
  • 11:30 a.m. UiPath (PATH) will host a live product webinar highlighting its latest agentic business orchestration capabilities, product strategy, and real-world customer outcomes.

Tuesday - April 7

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Levi Strauss (LEVI) and Greenbrier (GBX).
  • All day - The New York Federal Reserve will release its 1-Year Inflation Expectations figure report, derived from a monthly survey of consumers.
  • All day - The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index will be released.
  • All day - Nutanix (NTNX) will hold an Investor Day event in connection with the company's annual .NEXT user conference.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Durable Goods Orders report will be released by the Census Bureau.
  • 12:35 p.m. Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee will participate in a moderated Q&A at the Detroit Economic Club.

Wednesday - April 8

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Delta Air Lines (DAL), Constellation Brands (STZ), and PriceSmart (PSMT).
  • All day - FedEx (FDX) will hold an investor event focused on the FedEx Freight business ahead of the spinoff planned for June 1. Key topics could include margin improvement, network efficiency, and the role of data and automation in future growth
  • All day - Zscaler (ZS) will participate in the Wells Fargo 2026 Software Symposium in Menlo Park.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release the minutes from its last meeting.
  • Postmarket - Costco (COST) will issue its monthly sales report.

Thursday - April 9

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include WD-40 Company (WDFC) and Blackberry (BB).
  • All day - Dow (DOW) will hold its annual meeting. Shareholders will vote on new directors, a stock incentive plan amendment, and choosing the frequency of future say-on-pay votes.
  • All day - The USDA will issue its monthly World Agricultural Supply & Demand Estimates report. WASDE includes U.S. and world supply-demand tables for wheat, rice, coarse grains (such as corn, sorghum, barley, and oats), oilseeds (notably soybeans), and cotton, plus some coverage of sugar and livestock. Traders watch the April and May WASDE reports closely.
  • 8:30 a.m. The core PCE price index report for March will be released. Economists forecast an increase to 3.1% for the core rate.
  • 9:00 a.m. Home Depot (HD) CFO Richard McPhail will present at the J.P. Morgan Retail Round Up Forum.
  • 12:00 p.m. Walmart (WMT) CFO John Rainey will present at the J.P. Morgan Retail Round Up Forum.

Friday - April 10

  • All day - It is the FDA action date for Replimune's (REPL) RP1 in combination with nivolumab for the treatment of advanced melanoma in patients who progress on an anti-PD-1-containing regimen.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Consumer Price Index report for March will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Economists expect the core inflation rate to remain level at 2.5%.
  • 10:00 a.m. The U.S. Factory Orders report will be released by the Census Bureau.

Seeking Alpha editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article.

data.title: NeuroSense Therapeutics receives Nasdaq notices over listing requirements
data.description:
• NeuroSense Therapeutics (NRSN) on Friday said that it had received notifications from the Nasdaq Stock Market that it was not in compliance with minimum bid price and market value requirements for continued listing.
  • The company said its shares traded below the required minimum bid price of $1.00 for 30 consecutive business days between February 18 and March 31, and that its market value of listed securities also fell below the $35 million threshold over the same period.
  • NeuroSense said the notices have no immediate effect on the trading or listing of its securities, which will continue to trade on the exchange.
  • The company has until September 29, 2026, to regain compliance with both requirements, including maintaining a share price of at least $1.00 and a market value of at least $35 million for a minimum of 10 consecutive business days.
  • NRSN -3.95% after hours to $0.7972.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump administration considering expanding crackdown on Chinese tech gear - report
data.description:
The Federal Communications Commission ​is considering banning the ‌import of Chinese equipment from a group of manufacturers ​after previously barring approvals ​of new models in 2022, Reuters reported on Friday.

Earlier in 2021, the FCC added telecommunications and video surveillance ​equipment produced by Huawei Technologies, Hikvision, Hytera, Dahua and ZTE (ZTCOF) (ZTCOY) to its so-called "Covered List", citing national security risk. Then, in November ​2022, FCC said it would not ‌authorize ⁠the import or sale of new models from these Chinese companies.

As per the Reuters report, the FCC ​said ​ it was seeking comment on whether ​to bar the continued ​importation ⁠of equipment from the listed Chinese firms approved ⁠for ​sale in the ​U.S. before the 2022 order.

In 2025, the FCC also secured a majority vote on its proposal to impose new restrictions on Chinese companies that manufacture telecom equipment to ease national security concerns.

data.title: Trump threatens strikes on Iranian infrastructure as conflict escalates
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to target Iran’s bridges and power plants, saying the country’s leadership “knows what has to be done,” and that the United States military would hit Iran “extremely hard” for the next two to three weeks.

The warning comes after an airstrike destroyed a newly built bridge near Tehran, killing eight people, according to Iranian state media.

In a Truth Social post late on Thursday, Trump said the U.S. “hasn’t even started destroying what’s left in Iran. Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants!”

“New Regime leadership knows what has to be done, and has to be done, FAST!” Trump added without elaborating on what needed to be “done.”

In another post on Friday, Trump also suggested the U.S. could “open” the Strait of Hormuz and take control of oil flows.

Iran has effectively halted tanker traffic through the strait after attacks by U.S. and Israeli forces on February 28.

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi responded critically, questioning whether Americans wanted to “turn back the clock,” in reference to Trump’s remarks.

Meanwhile, operations at Abu Dhabi’s Hadshan gas facilities were suspended after debris from an intercepted strike caused a fire, with no reported injuries.

Legal experts warned that targeting civilian infrastructure such as power and water facilities could violate international law.

Separately, a proposed U.N. Security Council resolution to authorize  military action against Iran stalled after opposition from China, Russia, and France.

data.title: Quantum risk to crypto is advancing, Google paper warns
data.description:
Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) researchers have issued a new warning that future quantum computers may be capable of breaking the cryptography that protects Bitcoin (BTC-USD) (IBIT) and other cryptocurrencies with fewer resources than previously estimated.

In a recent blog post, Google Quantum AI researchers Ryan Babbush and Hartmut Neven revealed updated estimates showing an approximately 20-fold reduction in the quantum computing hardware needed to crack elliptic curve cryptography—the security foundation underpinning most crypto wallets and transactions.

The researchers said they have compiled quantum circuits that could solve the underlying mathematical problem, known as ECDLP-256, using fewer than 500,000 physical qubits and executing in just minutes. While no such machine exists today, the findings narrow the timeline for when quantum computers might pose a real threat to blockchain security.

“We urge all vulnerable cryptocurrency communities to join the migration to post-quantum cryptography without delay,” the researchers wrote in an accompanying white paper.

Google framed the disclosure as an early warning rather than a prediction of imminent danger, noting that the time remaining before such machines arrive still appears longer than what’s needed to transition public blockchains to quantum-resistant security. However, the “margin for error is increasingly narrow.”

The tech giant recently announced its own timeline to migrate all security systems to post-quantum cryptography by 2029. The company has been working on a responsible transition to quantum-safe standards since 2016.

To share the research responsibly, Google engaged with the U.S. government and developed a zero-knowledge proof method that allows third parties to verify the findings without providing a roadmap for potential attackers.

The researchers highlighted early efforts already underway across the industry, including post-quantum projects on Algorand (ALGO-USD), Solana (SOL-USD), and the XRP Ledger (XRP-USD), calling them evidence that “transition to post-quantum cryptography is realistic.”
